skill.name: enact-formatter
skill.description: Formats JSON strings with configurable indentation and validation feedback.
skill.summary: Prettifies JSON input and reports whether the input is valid.
skill.author: EnactProtocol

security_audit.summary: Static findings for weak cryptography and Markdown backticks were reviewed and dismissed as false positives. A separate medium-risk issue exists because SKILL.md passes user-controlled values into an unquoted command template, which may permit command injection depending on the Enact runtime.

medium_findings.title: Unquoted User Input in Command Template
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md defines a command string that inserts `${json}` and `${indent}` directly into a Node command. If the runtime executes this string through a shell, crafted JSON input could alter command arguments or execute shell metacharacters.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command template clearly includes user-controlled placeholders. Exploitability depends on runtime command execution semantics, so the risk is credible but not fully confirmed from these files alone.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Bash Fence
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution at the usage example is a Markdown code fence and example command text. It is documentation, not Ruby or shell backtick execution inside executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines show a fenced Markdown bash example. There is no executable Ruby code or backtick operator at this location.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Fence Boundary
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution near the end of the usage block is the closing Markdown fence. It does not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 31 is only the closing delimiter for a Markdown code block. No executable code path is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown JSON Fence
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution at the example output is a Markdown JSON code fence. It documents expected output and does not invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is a fenced JSON example in documentation. It contains no shell execution primitive.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Crypto Keywords
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are plain skill metadata and input descriptions. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API use appears at these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text for JSON formatting and indentation. No evidence of weak cryptographic algorithm use was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Template Uses Direct Placeholder Substitution
dangerous_patterns.description: The manifest command embeds `${json}` and `${indent}` directly into a command string. This pattern can be dangerous when untrusted input is evaluated by a shell or string-based command runner.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The risky substitution pattern is visible in the manifest. The available files do not prove whether the runtime shells out or safely tokenizes arguments.

content.value_statement: Raw JSON is hard to inspect when it arrives as one long string. This skill validates the input and returns readable formatting with a chosen indentation size.
actual_capabilities: Parses a JSON string and reports whether it is valid.
Formats valid JSON with configurable space indentation.
Returns formatted text together with a validation flag.
Reports parser error messages for invalid JSON input.
Runs through a small Node.js formatter script.
limitations: It only accepts JSON supplied as a string argument.
It does not repair invalid JSON automatically.
It does not sort keys, filter fields, or transform values.
Very large inputs may be limited by runtime argument size.

use_cases.title: Inspect API Responses
use_cases.description: Format compact API response bodies so fields and nesting are easier to review.

use_cases.title: Validate Configuration Snippets
use_cases.description: Check whether a JSON configuration snippet parses before sharing it with a team.

use_cases.title: Prepare Examples for Documentation
use_cases.description: Convert minified JSON examples into readable text for docs, tickets, or support notes.

prompt_templates.title: Format Basic JSON
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to format this JSON string with two-space indentation: [paste JSON string].

prompt_templates.title: Check Validity
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the formatter on this JSON and tell me whether it is valid. Explain any parser error in plain language: [paste JSON string].

prompt_templates.title: Change Indentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Format this JSON using four-space indentation and keep the original field values unchanged: [paste JSON string].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Documentation Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Format this compact JSON for a documentation example, then summarize the top-level fields without changing the data: [paste JSON string].

output_examples.input: A compact JSON object with a name field and a numeric value field.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a readable multi-line version and marks the input as valid.

output_examples.input: A JSON string with a missing closing brace.
output_examples.output: The skill returns no formatted value, marks the input as invalid, and includes the parser error message.

best_practices: Pass complete JSON strings and escape quotes according to your calling environment.
Use two spaces for general readability unless your project requires another indentation size.
Review invalid output messages before assuming the source data is corrupt.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to repair malformed JSON automatically.
Do not pass secrets unless your runtime and logs are approved for sensitive data.
Do not use it for non-JSON formats such as YAML, XML, or JavaScript objects.

faq.question: Does this skill validate JSON?
faq.answer: Yes. It parses the input and returns a validation status.

faq.question: Can I choose the indentation size?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill accepts an indent value and defaults to two spaces.

faq.question: Does it change field order or values?
faq.answer: No. It parses and reprints the JSON without intentional value transformations.

faq.question: Does it fix invalid JSON?
faq.answer: No. It reports parser errors but does not rewrite malformed input.

faq.question: Can it format large files?
faq.answer: It is designed for string input. Runtime argument limits may affect very large JSON content.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It can be published with a warning about direct command placeholder substitution in the manifest.
